questions.question: What component is responsible for temporarily holding data for quick access by the CPU?
Which tool is best for testing the voltage output of a power supply?
What type of expansion slot is commonly used for modern graphics cards?
What does BIOS stand for?
Which type of cable is used for high-speed network connections and has four twisted pairs?
What is the main purpose of thermal paste?
choices: Conduct heat between CPU and heatsink
questions.correct: Conduct heat between CPU and heatsink

questions.question: Which mobile connection type is best suited for high-speed data and global roaming?
What is the first step in the CompTIA troubleshooting methodology?
Which file system is used by default in Windows 10?
What command is used to check the current IP configuration in Windows?
What is a hypervisor in virtualization?
choices: A tool to manage hardware components
Software that allows multiple operating systems to run on a single physical machine
questions.correct: Software that allows multiple operating systems to run on a single physical machine

questions.question: Which type of memory is volatile and loses its data when power is removed?
True or False? Authentication is the process of verifying the identity of a user.
The four basic file attributes in most operating systems are:
Which port is commonly used for connecting a monitor to a modern PC?
Which protocol is used to send email from a client to a mail server?
Which cloud service model provides virtualized computing resources such as virtual machines and storage over the internet?
The data structure on a file system that tracks where files are physically stored on a disk is called the:
Which type of malware tricks a user into running it by appearing to be legitimate software, while performing malicious actions in the background?
Which server is responsible for resolving hostnames to IP addresses on a network?
Which server is responsible for dynamically assigning IP addresses to clients on a network?
What is the primary function of a GPU in a computer?
choices: To process graphics and video output
questions.correct: To process graphics and video output

questions.question: Which connector is used to connect internal storage drives to a motherboard?
A technician needs to securely wipe a PC before selling it. What is the BEST method?
choices: Use the OS reset option that removes all data and reinstalls Windows
questions.correct: Use the OS reset option that removes all data and reinstalls Windows

questions.question: Which component in a PC typically generates the most heat during sustained operation?
A technician suspects a RAM issue on a PC. Which symptom MOST strongly indicates a RAM problem?
choices: Continuous random reboots and blue screens
questions.correct: Continuous random reboots and blue screens

questions.question: Which technology would BEST allow a remote employee to securely access their company's internal network over the internet?
Which command is used to list the contents of a directory in Linux?
Which type of single-line comment syntax is used in JavaScript?
A central location for a Linux distribution's officially supported software packages is called a:
Which type of attack involves an attacker intercepting and potentially altering communications between two parties without their knowledge?
Which of the following BEST describes multi-factor authentication (MFA)?
choices: Logging in from multiple devices simultaneously
Requiring two or more verification factors from different categories to grant access
Using biometrics as the sole authentication method
questions.correct: Requiring two or more verification factors from different categories to grant access

questions.question: At which OSI layer does a router primarily operate?
Which OSI layer is responsible for end-to-end error recovery and flow control?
A host has the IP address 192.168.10.45/27. What is the network address of its subnet?
How many usable host addresses exist in a /26 subnet?
Which subnet mask is represented by /21?
Which command correctly assigns a switch port to VLAN 30?
What is the purpose of a trunk port on a switch?
choices: To carry traffic for a single VLAN only
To carry traffic for multiple VLANs between switches
To assign a port to the native VLAN
questions.correct: To carry traffic for multiple VLANs between switches

questions.question: Which STP port state forwards data frames AND learns MAC addresses?
Which bridge is elected as the root bridge in STP?
choices: The bridge with the highest MAC address
The bridge with the lowest bridge priority, then lowest MAC address
The bridge with the most ports
The bridge with the highest bridge priority
questions.correct: The bridge with the lowest bridge priority, then lowest MAC address

questions.question: What is the default administrative distance of OSPF?
Which routing protocol uses Dijkstra's Shortest Path First (SPF) algorithm?
What is the maximum hop count for RIP version 2?
A TCP segment has both the SYN and ACK flags set. What stage of the 3-way handshake does this represent?
choices: Step 2 – Server acknowledges and sends its own SYN
questions.correct: Step 2 – Server acknowledges and sends its own SYN

questions.question: Which protocol provides connectionless, best-effort delivery at the transport layer?
Which type of NAT maps multiple inside local addresses to a single public IP address using port numbers?
Where should a standard ACL be placed for optimal filtering efficiency?
choices: As close to the source as possible
As close to the destination as possible
questions.correct: As close to the destination as possible

questions.question: What is the correct order of messages in the DHCP lease process?
Which IPv6 address type is the equivalent of RFC 1918 private addresses in IPv4?
Which Cisco switch security feature prevents rogue DHCP servers by restricting DHCP replies to trusted ports only?
Which 802.11 standard introduced MIMO and operates on both 2.4 GHz and 5 GHz bands?
What type of malware disguises itself as legitimate software to trick users into installing it?
Which type of malware self-replicates and spreads across a network WITHOUT needing to attach itself to a host file?
What is the primary purpose of a rootkit?
choices: To encrypt files and demand a ransom
To gain and maintain privileged access while hiding its presence
To log keystrokes and steal credentials
questions.correct: To gain and maintain privileged access while hiding its presence

questions.question: Which of the following BEST describes a computer virus?
choices: Malware that spreads without user interaction
Malware that attaches itself to a host file and executes when that file is run
Malware that secretly records user activity
Malware that locks the user out of their system
questions.correct: Malware that attaches itself to a host file and executes when that file is run

questions.question: What distinguishes ransomware from other types of malware?
choices: It hides deep in the operating system kernel
It replicates itself across network shares
It encrypts a victim's data and demands payment for the decryption key
It silently monitors user behavior without causing damage
questions.correct: It encrypts a victim's data and demands payment for the decryption key

questions.question: A piece of malware is silently capturing everything a user types, including passwords and credit card numbers. What type of malware is this?
Which type of rootkit is the most difficult to detect and remove because it infects the Master Boot Record (MBR)?
What is a botnet?
choices: A network of firewalls protecting a system
A collection of infected machines controlled remotely by an attacker
A secure tunnel between two networks
questions.correct: A collection of infected machines controlled remotely by an attacker

questions.question: What is the key difference between spyware and adware?
choices: Adware encrypts files; spyware does not
Spyware collects user data secretly; adware primarily delivers unwanted advertisements
Adware spreads via networks; spyware requires physical access
Spyware is always more destructive than adware
questions.correct: Spyware collects user data secretly; adware primarily delivers unwanted advertisements

questions.question: What type of malware creates a hidden backdoor on an infected system, allowing an attacker to return at any time?
A logic bomb is best described as:
choices: Malware that spreads through email attachments
Malware that activates only when a specific condition or event is triggered
A type of rootkit targeting the BIOS
A virus that corrupts executable files
questions.correct: Malware that activates only when a specific condition or event is triggered

questions.question: What makes a polymorphic virus particularly difficult to detect with traditional antivirus software?
choices: It changes its code or signature each time it replicates
It hides in the system BIOS
It requires no host file to spread
questions.correct: It changes its code or signature each time it replicates

questions.question: A kernel-mode rootkit is more dangerous than a user-mode rootkit because:
choices: It operates with the highest level of system privileges, making it harder to detect
It is delivered only via phishing emails
questions.correct: It operates with the highest level of system privileges, making it harder to detect

questions.question: Which type of malware is specifically designed to mine cryptocurrency on a victim's machine without their knowledge?
Which of the following BEST describes the difference between a virus and a rootkit?
choices: A virus hides itself from the OS; a rootkit replicates by attaching to files
A virus replicates by attaching to files; a rootkit hides malicious activity and maintains privileged access
A rootkit always encrypts data; a virus does not
There is no difference — they are the same type of malware
questions.correct: A virus replicates by attaching to files; a rootkit hides malicious activity and maintains privileged access

questions.question: Which of the following is the MOST effective first step when responding to a ransomware infection?
choices: Reformat all drives on the network
Isolate the infected system from the network to prevent further spread
questions.correct: Isolate the infected system from the network to prevent further spread

questions.question: A fileless malware attack is dangerous primarily because:
choices: It always arrives via USB drives
It resides in memory and leaves little to no trace on disk, evading traditional file-based detection
It can only be stopped by reinstalling the OS
It exclusively targets mobile operating systems
questions.correct: It resides in memory and leaves little to no trace on disk, evading traditional file-based detection

questions.question: Which of the following BEST describes a classful routing protocol?
choices: It includes the subnet mask in its routing updates
It assumes all interfaces use the default subnet mask for their IP address class
It supports Variable Length Subnet Masking (VLSM)
It uses link-state advertisements to build a topology map
questions.correct: It assumes all interfaces use the default subnet mask for their IP address class

questions.question: Which of the following is a classful routing protocol?
What is the key advantage of a classless routing protocol over a classful one?
choices: It converges more slowly, giving routers time to sync
It includes the subnet mask in updates, supporting VLSM and CIDR
It uses hop count as its only metric
It requires less memory and CPU to operate
questions.correct: It includes the subnet mask in updates, supporting VLSM and CIDR

questions.question: A network uses the address 10.0.0.0 with a /20 subnet mask. Which type of routing protocol is REQUIRED to support this?
choices: Classful, because 10.0.0.0 is a Class A address
Classless, because the subnet mask differs from the default Class A mask of /8
Either classful or classless — both support custom masks
questions.correct: Classless, because the subnet mask differs from the default Class A mask of /8

questions.question: Which of the following is a defining characteristic of a distance-vector routing protocol?
choices: Each router maintains a full map of the network topology
Routers share their entire routing table with directly connected neighbors
Routers use Dijkstra's SPF algorithm to calculate paths
Routers send link-state advertisements to all routers in the area
questions.correct: Routers share their entire routing table with directly connected neighbors

questions.question: RIP is a distance-vector protocol. What metric does it use to determine the best path?
What problem can occur in distance-vector protocols when a route goes down and routers keep incrementing its metric back and forth?
Which distance-vector feature prevents a router from advertising a route back out the same interface it was learned on?
Which of the following BEST describes how a link-state routing protocol works?
choices: Each router sends its full routing table to all neighbors periodically
Each router builds a complete map of the network and independently calculates the best path
Routers only know the direction and distance to a destination via neighbors
Routes are assigned manually by a network administrator
questions.correct: Each router builds a complete map of the network and independently calculates the best path

questions.question: Which algorithm do link-state protocols use to calculate the shortest path to each destination?
Which algorithm do distance-vector protocols such as RIP use to calculate routing paths?
OSPF routers flood the network with what type of packet to share topology information?
Which of the following is an advantage of link-state protocols over distance-vector protocols?
choices: They use less CPU and memory
They converge faster because every router has a complete view of the topology
They do not require a designated router
questions.correct: They converge faster because every router has a complete view of the topology

questions.question: EIGRP is often described as an 'advanced distance-vector' or 'hybrid' protocol. What feature borrowed from link-state protocols does EIGRP use?
choices: It uses hop count as its sole metric
It sends full routing table updates every 30 seconds
It sends partial, bounded updates only when a topology change occurs
questions.correct: It sends partial, bounded updates only when a topology change occurs

questions.question: Which of the following correctly pairs a routing protocol with its type?
A router running a distance-vector protocol loses a route. What does a hold-down timer do?
choices: Immediately floods the network with the updated routing table
Prevents the router from accepting updates about that route for a set period, avoiding routing loops
Blocks all traffic to that destination permanently
questions.correct: Prevents the router from accepting updates about that route for a set period, avoiding routing loops

questions.question: Which of the following classful protocols was Cisco proprietary and has since been superseded by EIGRP?
In OSPF, what is the purpose of electing a Designated Router (DR) on a multi-access network segment?
choices: To assign IP addresses to other routers on the segment
To reduce LSA adjacencies by acting as a central point for link-state updates
To perform NAT for the local subnet
To act as the default gateway for end hosts
questions.correct: To reduce LSA adjacencies by acting as a central point for link-state updates

questions.question: Which statement BEST summarizes the difference between distance-vector and link-state routing protocols?
choices: Distance-vector routers know the full topology; link-state routers only know their neighbors
Distance-vector routers know direction and distance via neighbor tables; link-state routers build a full network map and calculate paths independently
Link-state protocols always use hop count; distance-vector protocols use bandwidth
There is no practical difference — both produce identical routing tables
questions.correct: Distance-vector routers know direction and distance via neighbor tables; link-state routers build a full network map and calculate paths independently

questions.question: Which command displays the last 20 lines of a file called 'syslog' in real time as it is being written to?
A Linux administrator wants to find all files owned by the user 'john' on the entire filesystem. Which command accomplishes this?
Which command would you use to change the ownership of a file called 'report.txt' to the user 'alice' and group 'finance'?
A file has the permissions -rwxr-x---. Which of the following BEST describes these permissions?
choices: Owner can read/write/execute; group can read/execute; others have no access
Owner can read/write; group can read/execute; others can execute
Owner can read/write/execute; group can read only; others have no access
Everyone can read and execute; only the owner can write
questions.correct: Owner can read/write/execute; group can read/execute; others have no access

questions.question: Which command is used to display currently running processes along with their CPU and memory usage in an interactive, real-time view?
A system administrator needs to schedule a script called 'backup.sh' to run every day at 2:30 AM. Which cron entry achieves this?
Which command would display the disk space usage of all mounted filesystems in a human-readable format?
A technician wants to search for the string 'error' recursively in all files under /var/log, ignoring case. Which command is correct?
Which file contains the hashed passwords for user accounts on a modern Linux system?
An administrator runs 'chmod 644 file.txt'. What are the resulting permissions?
Which command is used to display the routing table on a modern Linux system?
A process with PID 4823 is unresponsive and needs to be forcefully terminated. Which command achieves this?
